zh.lifeSummary: 當Hans Jacob Martin Wolff於1543年5月31日出生在德國爾特波格奧拉赤 斯圖特加特莫林根時，他的父親Georg Adam Gottlieb Wolff26歲，母親Lucia Schumacher23歲。他於1570年6月20日與Katharina Lucia Bientzlin在德國爾特波格奧拉赤 斯圖特加特莫林根結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1625年9月1日在德國巴登-爾特波格斯圖特加特莫林根享年82歲，埋葬在德國爾特波格奧拉赤 斯圖特加特莫林根。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hans Jacob Martin Wolff于1543年5月31日出生在德国爾特波格奧拉赤 斯圖特加特莫林根时，他的父亲Georg Adam Gottlieb Wolff26岁，母亲Lucia Schumacher23岁。他于1570年6月20日与Katharina Lucia Bientzlin在德国爾特波格奧拉赤 斯圖特加特莫林根结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1625年9月1日在德国巴登-爾特波格斯圖特加特莫林根享年82岁，埋葬在德国爾特波格奧拉赤 斯圖特加特莫林根。

nameMeanings.name: Wolff
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Fritz, Helmut, Franz, Gerhard, Guenter, Gunter, Heinz, Klaus.
German, Danish, English, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Wolf  .

Germanized or Americanized form of various other surnames meaning ‘wolf’, e.g. Hungarian   Farkas  , Czech and Slovak   Vlk  , Slovenian and other Slavic   Volk  ,   Vovk  , and   Vuk   (see   Wolf  ).

variants.name: Wolf
meanings: German, English, Dutch, and Danish: from a short form of the various ancient Germanic compound names with the first element wolf ‘wolf’, or a byname or nickname with this meaning, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a wolf. The wolf was native throughout the forests of Europe, including Britain, until comparatively recently. In ancient and medieval times it played an important role in ancient Germanic mythology, being regarded as one of the sacred beasts of Woden. The surname of German origin is also found in many other parts of Europe, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Hungary, Czechia, Slovakia, Slovenia, and Croatia, often as a German translation of local equivalents. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Farkas , Czech and Slovak Vlk , Slovenian Volk , Ukrainian and Slovenian Vovk , Slovenian, Croatian, and Serbian Vuk , and also Czech, Slovenian, Croatian, Slovak, and Hungarian Volf . In part, this is a Gottscheerish (i.e. Gottschee German) surname, originating from the Kočevsko region in Lower Carniola, Slovenia (see Kocevar ). Compare De Wolf , Wolfe , Wolff , Woolf , Woulfe , and Wulf .

Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish male personal name Volf meaning ‘wolf’, which is associated with the Hebrew personal name Binyamin (see Benjamin ). This association stems from Jacob's dying words ‘Benjamin shall ravin as a wolf: in the morning he shall devour the prey, and at night he shall divide the spoil’ (Genesis 49:27). Compare Volf .

nameMeanings.name: Hans
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Jacob
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, from Hebrew Yaakov. This was borne by perhaps the most important of all the patriarchs in the Book of Genesis. Jacob was the father of twelve sons, who gave their names to the twelve tribes of Israel. He was the son of Isaac and Rebecca and twin brother of Esau. According to the story in Genesis, he was the cunning younger twin, who persuaded his brother Esau to part with his right to his inheritance in exchange for a bowl of soup (‘a mess of pottage’). Later, he tricked his blind and dying father into blessing him in place of Esau. The derivation of the name has been much discussed. It is traditionally explained as being derived from Hebrew akev ‘heel’ and to have meant ‘heel grabber’, because when Jacob was born ‘his hand took hold of Esau's heel’ (Genesis 25:26). This is interpreted later in the Bible as ‘supplanter’ Esau himself remarks, ‘Is he not rightly named Jacob? for he has supplanted me these two times’ (Genesis 27:36). Jacob is especially common as a Jewish given name, although it also became very popular among the Puritans from the 16th century onwards, and has again been widely used since the 1990s. Compare James .

nameMeanings.name: Martin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the Latin name Martinus. This was probably originally derived from Mars (genitive Martis), the name of the Roman god of war (and earlier of fertility). Martin became very popular in the Middle Ages, especially on the Continent, as a result of the fame of St Martin of Tours . He was born the son of a Roman officer in Upper Pannonia (an outpost of the Roman Empire, now part of Hungary), and, although he became a leading figure in the 4th-century Church, he is chiefly remembered now for having divided his cloak in two and given half to a beggar. The name was also borne by five popes, including one who defended Roman Catholic dogma against Eastern Orthodox theology. He died after suffering imprisonment and privations in Naxos and public humiliation in Constantinople, and was promptly acclaimed a martyr by supporters of the Roman Church. Among Protestants, the name is sometimes bestowed in honour of the German theologian Martin Luther ( 1483–1546 ); Martin was used as a symbolic name for the Protestant Church in satires by both Dryden and Swift. A further influence may be its use as the given name of the civil-rights leader Martin Luther King ( 1929–68 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Hans Jacob Martin Wolff was born on 31 May 1543, in Möhringen, Stuttgart, Württemberg, Germany, his father, Georg Adam Gottlieb Wolff, was 26 and his mother, Lucia Schumacher, was 23. He married Katharina Lucia Bientzlin on 20 June 1570, in Möhringen, Stuttgart, Württemberg, Germany. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 1 September 1625, in Möhringen, Stuttgart, Baden-Württemberg, Germany, at the age of 82, and was buried in Möhringen, Stuttgart, Württemberg, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hans Jacob Martin Wolff est né le 31 mai 1543, à Möhringen, Oberamt Stuttgart, Württemberg, Allemagne, son père, Georg Adam Gottlieb Wolff, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Lucia Schumacher, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Katharina Lucia Bientzlin le 20 juin 1570, à Möhringen, Oberamt Stuttgart, Württemberg, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 1 septembre 1625, à Möhringen, Stuttgart, Baden-Württemberg, Allemagne, à l’âge de 82 ans, et a été enterré à Möhringen, Oberamt Stuttgart, Württemberg, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hans Jacob Martin Wolff am 31. Mai 1543 in Möhringen, Oberamt Stuttgart, Württemberg, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Georg Adam Gottlieb Wolff 26 und seine Mutter Lucia Schumacher 23. Er hat am 20. Juni 1570 in Möhringen, Oberamt Stuttgart, Württemberg, Deutschland Katharina Lucia Bientzlin geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 1. September 1625 im Alter von 82 Jahren in Möhringen, Stuttgart, Baden-Württemberg, Deutschland und wurde in Möhringen, Oberamt Stuttgart, Württemberg, Deutschland begraben.

Lui sposato Katharina Lucia Bientzlin il 20 giugno 1570, nel Möhringen, Oberamt Stuttgart, Württemberg, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Hans Jacob Martin Wolffが1543年5月31日にドイツウuルッテンバーグオバーアムト ストゥットガートムohリンジェンで生まれた時，父Georg Adam Gottlieb Wolffは26歳，母Lucia Schumacherは23歳であった。1570年6月20日にドイツウuルッテンバーグオバーアムト ストゥットガートムohリンジェンでKatharina Lucia Bientzlinと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1625年9月1日にドイツバデン-ウuルッテンバーグストゥットガートムohリンジェンで82歳で死去し，ドイツウuルッテンバーグオバーアムト ストゥットガートムohリンジェンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hans Jacob Martin Wolff(이)가 1543년 5월 31일 독일 wu르테버그 오베램트 스투트가트 oh링에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Georg Adam Gottlieb Wolff(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Lucia Schumacher(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1570년 6월 20일 독일 wu르테버그 오베램트 스투트가트 oh링에서 Katharina Lucia Bientzlin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1625년 9월 1일 독일 바데-wu르테버그 스투트가트 oh링에서 82세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 wu르테버그 오베램트 스투트가트 oh링에 묻혔다.

pt.31. Mai 1543–1. September 1625: 31 de maio de 1543–1 de setembro de 1625
pt.Möhringen, Unlingen, Riedlingen, Württem: Möhringen, Unlingen, Oberamt Riedlingen, Württemberg, Alemanha
Staffort, Stutensee, Landkreis Karlsruhe, Baden-Württemberg, Alemanha
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hans Jacob Martin Wolff nasceu em 31 de maio de 1543, em Möhringen, Oberamt Stuttgart, Württemberg, Alemanha, seu pai, Georg Adam Gottlieb Wolff, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Lucia Schumacher, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Katharina Lucia Bientzlin em 20 de junho de 1570, em Möhringen, Oberamt Stuttgart, Württemberg, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1 de setembro de 1625, em Möhringen, Stuttgart, Baden-Württemberg, Alemanha, com 82 anos, e foi sepultado em Möhringen, Oberamt Stuttgart, Württemberg, Alemanha.

ru.Möhringen, Unlingen, Riedlingen, Württem: мoхринген, унлинген, обирамт риедлинген, вuрттембирг, Германия
стаффорт, стутенси, ландкрис карлсрух, баден-вuрттембирг, Германия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hans Jacob Martin Wolff родился 31 мая 1543 г. в местности мoхринген, обирамт стуттгарт, вuрттембирг, Германия, его отцу по имени Georg Adam Gottlieb Wolff было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Lucia Schumacher, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Katharina Lucia Bientzlin 20 июня 1570 г. в местности мoхринген, обирамт стуттгарт, вuрттембирг, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 1 сентября 1625 г. в местности мoхринген, стуттгарт, баден-вuрттембирг, Германия в возрасте 82 лет и был похоронен в местности мoхринген, обирамт стуттгарт, вuрттембирг, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hans Jacob Martin Wolff nació el 31 de mayo de 1543, en Möhringen, Oberamt Stuttgart, Württemberg, Alemania, su papá, Georg Adam Gottlieb Wolff, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Lucia Schumacher, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Katharina Lucia Bientzlin el 20 de junio de 1570, en Möhringen, Oberamt Stuttgart, Württemberg, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 1 de septiembre de 1625, en Möhringen, Stuttgart, Baden-Württemberg, Alemania, a la edad de 82 años, y fue sepultado en Möhringen, Oberamt Stuttgart, Württemberg, Alemania.
